sheets.name: README
rows: Defines the 20 dimensions for dish tagging and how they are used for ranking and personalization after class-first planning.
Generate weekly meal classes before dish recommendations.
Keep primary family meals separate from member-specific add-ons.
Do not invent new IDs in application code; manage changes through DOC-27/DOC-28.
Apply hard constraints before scoring and personalization.

sheets.name: Food_DNA_Dimensions
rows: north_indian, south_indian, bengali, gujarati, maharashtrian, etc.
gluten, dairy, nuts, soy, shellfish, egg, none
fried, steamed, sauteed, boiled, pressure_cooked, tandoor
soft, crispy, creamy, crunchy, chewy, dry

sheets.name: Auto_Derive_Rules
rows: Recipe steps contain steam, fry, saute, pressure cook.
Chilli count, regional default, recipe notes.
Crispy/soft/creamy from method and dish knowledge.
Curd/cold/light for hot; fried/chai/khichdi for rainy.

sheets.name: Sample_Tag_Template
rows: Cross-cuisine match for fermented light breakfast.

sheets.name: Dish_Source_Lookup
